description: A learned, repeatable capacity for practical work is 'thesani'. Skill stays with the person between uses and outside any institution's approval. 'pokera' (competent) asks whether someone can meet a present criterion; skill is the capacity built across occasions.
articulatory_notes: For unvoiced dental 'th', let breath pass between the tongue tip and upper teeth into true-mid front 'e'. Narrow the channel at the alveolar ridge for 's', then open into stressed central 'a'. Return to the teeth for voiced nasal 'n' and finish on high-front unrounded 'i'.
sound_symbolism: Breath changes from the dental opening of 'the' to the finer hiss of stressed 'sa', then settles into the nasal contact of 'ni'. Three controlled air paths echo a capacity refined through distinct motions.
usage_notes: The noun may identify one skill or practical skill in general. The person holds it with 'phelu'. Its domain, tools, and present conditions matter when they alter the claim.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Craft skill joins technique with attention to material, form, and use. Ornament can grow from how the object is made.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Skill can be recognised without turning it into authority over another person or a measure of human worth.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Embodied skill carries repair and making across generations through demonstration, practice, and shared work.

semantic_domains.activity: learned capacity for repeatable practical action
semantic_domains.creation: capacity applied in making, repair, and transformation
semantic_domains.wisdom: practical understanding refined across repeated experience
